behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is module handoff (collectors -> aggregator -> summary input). Unit is too low because single-function tests can miss dropped/overwritten sections during wiring. System was considered but rejected because behavior text is about "content passed" and can be validated with in-process source fakes; no real OS/network requirement. Agentic was considered but rejected because this checks pre-summary payload, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real orchestration path with three deterministic source adapters (or fakes) returning distinct markers, capture the payload passed into summary generation, and assert it includes all three source sections and key fields.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears when source-client error handling, aggregation, and report rendering interact. Unit is too low because this is cross-module fallback behavior. System was considered but rejected: "one data source is unavailable" can be reproduced by adapter exceptions/timeouts in-process; real network outage is not required by the behavior text. Agentic/Workflow rejected because correctness is fallback plumbing, not LLM decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real report assembly with one source adapter failing and two succeeding, then assert report generation completes and includes a section-scoped `data unavailable` note instead of terminating.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on what the summary generator actually produces from data; stubs/mocks can always pass and hide hallucination/omission failures. This is the deterministic-assertion trap: even with fixed checks, real model judgment is what is being tested. Workflow was considered but rejected because behavior text describes one summary-generation step, not multiple agentic decisions chained together.
behaviors.test_description: Use representative real input datasets, call the production summary generator, and verify the produced narrative preserves critical values/facts (with tolerant parsing/rubric) across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real delivery infrastructure and inbox reception; mocks can hide SMTP/API auth, transport, encoding, and recipient-routing issues. Integration is too low for "reaches ... inbox correctly." Agentic was considered but rejected because this behavior text is about end-to-end transport/delivery success, not evaluating model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the script end-to-end against test instances of the three data sources plus a real test mail service/inbox, then assert the configured recipient mailbox receives the report with expected structure.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior spans real persistence and cross-run retry semantics. In-process mocks can miss file path/permission/serialization issues and state reload between runs. System is needed to exercise unreachable delivery endpoint + filesystem + next-run execution boundary. Agentic/Workflow rejected because fallback and retry logic do not depend on model decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run 1: execute script with mail endpoint unreachable (real connection failure), assert report is written to fallback file. Run 2 (new process/next invocation): restore mail service, execute again, assert queued report is delivered and fallback artifact is cleared/marked sent.
